title: Planning workflow robustness for issues #666-#672
goal: Implement the post-feedback planning robustness issues #666 through #672 so agents can create valid plans, recover from guessed planning shapes, and use schema-backed decomposition for epic-shaped work.
non_goals: Do not remove existing bootstrap CLI names or break compatibility aliases in this lane.
Do not turn Agentic Workspace into a general project-management system.

intent.outcome: Make the valid planning path cheaper than freehanding invalid planning artifacts.
intent.constraints: Stay provider-neutral and preserve checked-in planning as execution authority.
intent.latitude: Implement safe CLI, schema, checker, summary, and documentation changes needed for the issue set.
intent.escalation: Stop if naming migration requires breaking public commands.

execution.next_step: Archive this execplan and close issues #666-#672 after commit/push.
execution.proof: Run focused planning/root CLI tests, planning surface checks, maintainer surface refresh where guidance changes, and make check before closeout.

invariants: Summary/preflight must not report clean idle when unrecognized active intent is present.
Generated/scaffolded records must validate against shipped schemas.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Agentic Workspace should funnel large agent work into reliable planning before implementation and make bypass/freehand mistakes visible.
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: partial but intended to close issues #666-#672
intent_continuity.continuation surface: GitHub issues #666-#672 plus this execplan closeout

required_continuation.required follow-on for the larger intend: Any discovered dogfooding friction should become narrow follow-up issues unless it is an immediate bug in this lane.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Ingest and implement the issues created since the feedback.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Close the workflow gaps exposed by the external agent's invalid planning artifacts.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Implement the seven created issues as one planning robustness lane.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check whether the changes make the correct workflow easier and invalid workflow harder.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning package, root workspace CLI, shipped planning payload, tests, docs/reference when needed
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused pytest, planning surface checker, make check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: breaking CLI/package rename or broad state model replacement

stop_conditions.stop when: public command renaming would break compatibility or issue scope requires a larger architecture decision
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: create a follow-up issue and report it
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: do not add unrelated planning product work
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: fix failing focused tests before continuing

context_budget.live working set: issue bodies, planning CLI/installer/checker/schema code, root CLI command wiring, tests
context_budget.recoverable later: full archive history and unrelated package docs
context_budget.externalize before shift: record dogfooding findings as GitHub issues
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: not required unless code context points to a durable invariant
context_budget.tiny resumability note: continue from this execplan and summary output
context_budget.context-shift triggers: large naming migration or decomposition model uncertainty

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Implement the issue set without waiting for additional chat clarification.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Commit and push as work progresses; use package workflow; close/fix actual bugs immediately.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact schema field names, command help wording, and test organization.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A public breaking rename is needed.

adaptive_assurance.reason: This touches startup/planning funnel behavior and shipped package surfaces that exist to prevent agent mistakes.

acceptance_refs: #666 implemented by new-plan scaffold command and package tests.
#667 implemented by top-level planning help command and operation contract.
#668 implemented by checker warnings for generic PM-shaped execplans.
#669 implemented by bootstrap naming clarification and naming note.
#670 implemented by WORKFLOW guidance updates clarifying CLI-first orientation versus package-owned mutations.
#671 implemented by unsupported state activation shape detection in checker and installed summary.
#672 implemented by schema-backed decomposition artifacts, projection, payload packaging, and unsupported epic Markdown warning.

active_milestone.scope: Implement the accepted issue cluster and update planning state/proof.
active_milestone.ready: issue bodies and current planning code inspected

immediate_next_action: Implement the new-plan scaffold, planning help router, malformed-shape detection, unsupported state-shape detection, decomposition schema/projection, and guidance updates.
invariants: Checked-in planning remains the execution authority.
Package commands should create or normalize planning structure when available.
Direct file edits are bounded fallback/content edits, not permission to invent schema shape.
open_questions_to_close: How minimal can the first decomposition artifact be while still preventing unmanaged epic Markdown drift?
What first safe naming slice clarifies bootstrap lifecycle responsibility without breaking users?
validation_commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q
completion_criteria: #666-#672 acceptance criteria are implemented or narrowed into explicit follow-up issues where the issue itself calls for migration planning.
Dogfooding friction is fixed immediately if it is an actual bug, otherwise routed into issues.
Changes are committed and pushed to master.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented issues #666-#672: new-plan scaffold, planning help, malformed execplan/state detection, first-class decomposition artifacts, CLI-first guidance, naming note, tests, and operation contracts.
execution_run.scope touched: planning package, root workspace CLI, shipped planning payload, contracts, docs, and focused tests
execution_run.changed surfaces: planning package source/checker/tests, root CLI/contracts/tests, planning payload templates/schemas/guidance, and docs/planning-cli-naming.md
execution_run.validations run: focused pytest suites, new-plan temp-repo smoke test, contract tooling, maintainer surfaces, planning surface checker, make check
execution_run.result for continuation: archive lane after commit and close issues #666-#672

proof_report.validation proof: make check passed after focused tests, contract tooling, maintainer surfaces, planning surface checker, and new-plan smoke test.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: make check exited 0; focused test groups passed; temp installed workspace created and activated a new schema-backed plan.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Ingest and implement the issues we have created since the feedback.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Implemented issues #666-#672: new-plan scaffold, planning help, invalid execplan/state detection, decomposition artifacts, guidance, naming notes, tests, and operation contracts. make check passed.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: Issue cluster implementation and proof are complete; remaining broad external-work snapshot warnings are unrelated repository backlog.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: make check passed; focused tests and smoke test passed; planning surface checker clean.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if generated planning scaffolds fail validation, planning help is missing, or summary again reports idle for unsupported active state.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Planning lifecycle mutations should prefer package commands, while direct file edits remain bounded content edits inside schema-backed records.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Captured in WORKFLOW.md, planning help, decomposition README/template, and docs/planning-cli-naming.md.
durable_residue.canonical owner now: shipped planning guidance and CLI help

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Planning workflow robustness issue cluster #666-#672 implemented.
execution_summary.resume from: no active continuation for this lane

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Operation contract parity caught the new planning help command only during make check.
signals fixed: Added planning.help operation contract and planning.help.build primitive.
signals dismissed: Closeout-trust lower-trust signal comes from pre-existing external-work snapshot backlog unrelated to this lane.

discard.summary: No additional dogfooding issue created; lane-local friction was fixed in this patch.

memory_learning_capture.target: shipped planning guidance and CLI help
